metadata.title: Dissociation of Connectivity for Syntactic Irregularity and Perceptual Ambiguity in Musical Chord Stimuli
metadata.note: Original MEG study examining dissociation of syntactic irregularity and perceptual ambiguity in musical chord stimuli using effective connectivity (linearized time-delayed mutual information, LTDMI). Three conditions: T (dominant to tonic, most regular), SM (dominant to submediant, less irregular, most ambiguous), ST (dominant to supertonic, most irregular).

claims.claim_text: Connectivity from the right to the left IFG (IFG-LTDMI) was enhanced for the ST (most irregular condition) compared to T (F(2,36)=6.526, p=0.024, FDR corrected; post hoc: ST > T, t(18)=3.091, p=0.009; ST > SM, t(18)=3.223, p=0.009), suggesting that IFG-LTDMI reflects syntactic irregularity processing

dependent_variable.name: IFG-LTDMI (effective connectivity from right to left IFG)

axis_proposal.definition: Effective connectivity from right to left inferior frontal gyrus (IFG) measured using linearized time-delayed mutual information (LTDMI), reflecting syntactic irregularity processing in musical chord sequences
axis_proposal.psychoacoustic_basis: LTDMI is information-theoretic measure of functional coupling based on mutual information, predicting information transmission between two time series. Higher IFG-LTDMI for most irregular condition (ST) suggests enhanced interhemispheric connectivity in IFG for syntactic irregularity processing.

statistics.post_hoc: ST > T: t(18)=3.091, p=0.009 (FDR corrected); ST > SM: t(18)=3.223, p=0.009 (FDR corrected); T vs SM: t(18)=0.061, p=0.952 (FDR corrected)
statistics.note: IFG-LTDMI values: T=0.1584±0.1207, SM=0.1563±0.1116, ST=0.2614±0.1186. Highest IFG-LTDMI for ST (most irregular condition). This extends previous ERAN findings showing ERAN only for most irregular condition (ST). IFG-LTDMI in terms of effective connectivity may underlie ERAN.

brain_regions.mni_coordinates: Talairach coordinates: left IFG -40.8, 18.5, 15.6 mm; right IFG 37.6, 21.2, 15.1 mm
brain_regions.context: Multiple equivalent current dipoles (ECDs) for bilateral IFGs fitted on generators of ERANm (magnetic counterpart of ERAN) in 140-220 ms for all ending chords. Estimated dipoles in IFG were superior and anterior to those in STG. Goodness of fit >80%.

methodology.task: Participants listened to five-chord sequences with three different endings: T (dominant to tonic, most regular), SM (dominant to submediant, less irregular), ST (dominant to supertonic, most irregular). Each chord 600 ms, sequence total 3600 ms. Participants detected staccato chords to maintain attention.
methodology.control_conditions: Within-subjects design. Three conditions: T, SM, ST. All conditions transposed into 12 major keys, randomly shuffled. First four chords same in all conditions, only ending chord differed.
methodology.participant_characteristics: 19 female participants (mean age 24.3±3.0 years): 9 musicians, 10 non-musicians. Musicians majored in musical instruments, received training from age 5 for at least 15 years. Normal hearing, right-handed. Only women recruited due to gender differences in hemispheric dominance of syntactic processing.
methodology.analysis_details: MEG recorded with 306-channel whole-head system (Elekta NeuroMag VectorView). Source analysis of four ROIs (bilateral IFGs and STGs) using BESA. ECDs for bilateral IFGs fitted on ERANm generators. ECD signals extracted in 400-ms epochs after ending chord onset, 1-20 Hz bandpass filter. Effective connectivity calculated by LTDMI with 120 ms delay time.

claims.claim_text: Connectivity from the right to the left STG (STG-LTDMI) was enhanced for the SM (most ambiguous condition) compared to T and ST (F(2,36)=12.373, p<0.001, FDR corrected; post hoc: SM > T, t(18)=2.691, p=0.023; SM > ST, t(18)=5.357, p=0.0001; T > ST, t(18)=2.259, p=0.037), suggesting that STG-LTDMI reflects perceptual ambiguity processing

dependent_variable.name: STG-LTDMI (effective connectivity from right to left STG)

axis_proposal.definition: Effective connectivity from right to left superior temporal gyrus (STG) measured using linearized time-delayed mutual information (LTDMI), reflecting perceptual ambiguity processing in musical chord sequences
axis_proposal.psychoacoustic_basis: LTDMI measures information transmission between time series. Higher STG-LTDMI for most ambiguous condition (SM) suggests enhanced interhemispheric connectivity in STG for perceptual ambiguity processing. STG is related to acoustic-phonetic processing, pitch and melody processing, and processing of ambiguous stimuli.

statistics.post_hoc: SM > T: t(18)=2.691, p=0.023 (FDR corrected); SM > ST: t(18)=5.357, p=0.0001 (FDR corrected); T > ST: t(18)=2.259, p=0.037 (FDR corrected)
statistics.note: STG-LTDMI values: T=0.2019±0.0919, SM=0.2500±0.0843, ST=0.1438±0.0541. Highest STG-LTDMI for SM (most ambiguous condition). Questionnaire: 66.6% of respondents felt most uncertain when discriminating SM, while 13 responded 'certain' for ST. Patterns consistent with hypothesis that SM would be most ambiguous.

brain_regions.mni_coordinates: Talairach coordinates: left STG -45.1, -8.9, 1.9 mm; right STG 43.1, -2.6, 2.0 mm
brain_regions.context: Multiple ECDs for bilateral STGs fitted on generators of P2m (magnetic counterpart of P2) in peak latency 180-190 ms for average of all in-key chords. ECDs localized on bilateral STGs involving auditory cortices. Goodness of fit >80%.

methodology.task: Same as C01. Three conditions: T (most regular), SM (less irregular, most ambiguous), ST (most irregular).
methodology.analysis_details: Same as C01. ECDs for bilateral STGs fitted on P2m generators. ECD signals extracted in 400-ms epochs after ending chord onset, 1-20 Hz bandpass filter. Effective connectivity calculated by LTDMI with 120 ms delay time.

claims.claim_text: Correct rate (CR) was negatively correlated with STG-LTDMI (Spearman's ρ=-0.260, p=0.026, one-tailed, FDR corrected), but not with IFG-LTDMI (ρ=0.064, p=0.319), further reflecting that STG-LTDMI reflects perceptual ambiguity processing

independent_variable.name: STG-LTDMI (effective connectivity from right to left STG)

dependent_variable.name: Correct rate (CR) in behavioral discrimination task

statistics.p_value: STG-LTDMI: ρ=-0.260, p=0.026 (one-tailed, FDR corrected); IFG-LTDMI: ρ=0.064, p=0.319
statistics.note: Correlation tested using values for all conditions and participants (n=57, 3 conditions × 19 participants). One-tailed test because ambiguous stimuli lead to slower and less accurate responses. Mean CR: T=82.4%, SM=77.0%, ST=88.7%. SM significantly lower than ST (t(17)=-2.574, p=0.020). Negative correlation suggests higher STG-LTDMI (more ambiguous) associated with lower CR (less accurate).

brain_regions.mni_coordinates: Talairach coordinates: left STG -45.1, -8.9, 1.9 mm; right STG 43.1, -2.6, 2.0 mm
brain_regions.context: Same as C02. STG-LTDMI reflects perceptual ambiguity processing, with negative correlation to correct rate.

methodology.task: MEG experiment: participants detected staccato chords. Behavioral experiment: participants discriminated three conditions (T, SM, ST) using 1, 2, 3 buttons on keypad. 12 sequences per condition randomly presented.
methodology.control_conditions: Within-subjects design. Three conditions: T, SM, ST.
methodology.participant_characteristics: Same as C01. One outlier excluded from behavioral analysis.
methodology.analysis_details: Correlation analysis between LTDMI values and CR for all conditions and participants (n=57). Spearman's rank correlation used because data not normally distributed. One-tailed test. FDR correction for multiple comparisons.

c3_associations.process: Predictive coding of musical syntax, with IFG-LTDMI (effective connectivity from right to left IFG) enhanced for most irregular condition (ST), reflecting syntactic irregularity processing and underlying ERAN

brain_regions.role: Syntactic irregularity processing, with enhanced interhemispheric connectivity (right to left) for most irregular chord sequences

c3_associations.evidence: IFG-LTDMI enhanced for ST (most irregular condition: dominant to supertonic) compared to T (most regular: dominant to tonic) and SM (less irregular: dominant to submediant). This extends previous ERAN findings showing ERAN only for most irregular condition. IFG-LTDMI in terms of effective connectivity may underlie ERAN, which reflects expectation violations in chordal sequences.

c3_associations.process: Spectral processing of perceptual ambiguity, with STG-LTDMI (effective connectivity from right to left STG) enhanced for most ambiguous condition (SM), reflecting uncertainty in judgment/classification of presented stimuli

brain_regions.role: Perceptual ambiguity processing, with enhanced interhemispheric connectivity (right to left) for most ambiguous chord sequences

c3_associations.evidence: STG-LTDMI enhanced for SM (most ambiguous condition: dominant to submediant) compared to T and ST. Correct rate negatively correlated with STG-LTDMI (ρ=-0.260, p=0.026), but not with IFG-LTDMI. Questionnaire: 66.6% of respondents felt most uncertain when discriminating SM. STG is related to acoustic-phonetic processing, pitch and melody processing, and processing of ambiguous stimuli.

r3_coordinates.note: Three five-chord sequences: T (tonic-submediant-supertonic-dominant-tonic, most regular), SM (ending with submediant, less irregular, most ambiguous), ST (ending with supertonic, most irregular). Each chord 600 ms, sequence total 3600 ms including 600 ms resting period. Recorded at 100 BPM. All conditions transposed into 12 major keys. Piano timbre (Bösendorfer 290 Imperial grand). No specific frequency or tempo parameters reported, as study focuses on harmonic relationships and syntactic irregularity/perceptual ambiguity rather than spectral or temporal features.

mentions.context: Multiple ECDs for bilateral IFGs fitted on generators of ERANm in 140-220 ms for all ending chords. Estimated dipoles in IFG were superior and anterior to those in STG. Talairach coordinates: left IFG -40.8, 18.5, 15.6 mm; right IFG 37.6, 21.2, 15.1 mm
Syntactic irregularity processing, with enhanced interhemispheric connectivity (right to left) for most irregular chord sequences

Multiple ECDs for bilateral STGs fitted on generators of P2m in peak latency 180-190 ms for average of all in-key chords. ECDs localized on bilateral STGs involving auditory cortices. Talairach coordinates: left STG -45.1, -8.9, 1.9 mm; right STG 43.1, -2.6, 2.0 mm
STG-LTDMI reflects perceptual ambiguity processing, with negative correlation to correct rate
Perceptual ambiguity processing, with enhanced interhemispheric connectivity (right to left) for most ambiguous chord sequences

brain_regions_summary.note: All brain regions identified through MEG source localization using BESA with multiple equivalent current dipoles (ECDs). Four ROIs: bilateral IFGs and STGs. ECDs for bilateral IFGs fitted on ERANm generators (140-220 ms). ECDs for bilateral STGs fitted on P2m generators (180-190 ms). Goodness of fit >80%. Talairach coordinates provided for all regions. Effective connectivity calculated using linearized time-delayed mutual information (LTDMI) with 120 ms delay time.

warnings: 2 DV r3_axis unmapped (axis_proposal provided for all)
MEG source localization with Talairach coordinates (not MNI, but acceptable)
Effective connectivity measure (LTDMI) rather than direct neural activity
No specific frequency or tempo parameters (focus on harmonic relationships)

data_quality.note: Original MEG study with 19 female participants (9 musicians, 10 non-musicians). All statistics reported (repeated measures ANOVA, post hoc t-tests, Spearman correlation, p-values, F-values). Brain regions identified through MEG source localization (Talairach coordinates). Effective connectivity measured using LTDMI. R³ coordinates not mappable as study focuses on harmonic relationships and syntactic irregularity/perceptual ambiguity rather than spectral or temporal features. Chord sequences at 100 BPM, but no specific frequency parameters reported.
